---
title: "Using AI to Analyze and Strengthen Your Business"
subtitle: "Capital Readiness Program for Women Entrepreneurs"
author: "Dr. Logan Kelly"
institute: "University of Wisconsin–River Falls"
date: "April 14, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [white, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    footer: "Dr. Logan Kelly | UWRF | April 14, 2026"
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    embed-resources: true
---

## Today's Plan

1. **C.O.R.E. Framework** — A structured way to get useful output from AI
2. **Live Demo** — Walk through C.O.R.E. on real business data
3. **Your Action Items** — Three things to try this week

---

## The C.O.R.E. Framework

| Component | What It Means | What You Do |
|-----------|--------------|-------------|
| **C**ontext | Situational awareness | Feed AI your actual business data |
| **O**bjective | Strategic intent | Tell AI exactly what you want |
| **R**easoning | Multi-step logic | Make AI show its work |
| **E**valuation | Risk & validation | Ask AI to critique itself |

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026). "How FP&A Professionals Should Prompt AI in 2026." *FP&A Trends*.
:::

---

## C — Context: Feed AI Your Data

> "Context Engineering" is superseding prompt engineering as the critical skill for 2026.

- Upload your **actual business files** — invoices, reports, records
- AI establishes a "ground truth" from your data
- Modern AI can process thousands of rows instantly

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026); Stellium Consulting (2026); Gonzalez de Villaumbrosia (2026).
:::

---

## O — Objective: Say Exactly What You Want

The **D.A.T.A.** framework for direct navigation:

| Step | What It Means | Example |
|------|--------------|---------|
| **D**ata Source | Which file or tab | "Use the January invoice export" |
| **A**ction | The sharp goal | "Identify top 5 late-paying industries" |
| **T**echnical Constraints | What to exclude | "Ignore credit memos" |
| **A**nswer Style | Output format | "Return a ranked table" |

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026); McKinsey (2025) — only 1% of companies considered AI "mature."
:::

---

## R — Reasoning: Make AI Show Its Work

- Prompt: **"Think step-by-step"** — forces auditable logic
- AI shifts from "template filler" to **reasoning partner**
- Chain of thought reduces hallucinations

**Cautionary tale:** Deloitte Australia refunded part of an AU$440,000 contract — AI-generated report contained fabricated court quotes.

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026); Probert (2026). "How AI Is Transforming the Consulting Industry." *Whitehat SEO*.
:::

---

## E — Evaluation: Verify Everything

- Ask: **"Critique your own analysis. What are the limitations?"**
- Human-in-the-loop is **non-negotiable**
- EU AI Act takes effect August 2, 2026

::: {.aside}
Gonzalez de Villaumbrosia (2026); Jasimuddin (2026).
:::

---

## Six AI Risks Every Business Should Know

| Risk | What It Means for You |
|------|----------------------|
| **Adversarial input** | Carefully worded prompts can trick AI into leaking your data or ignoring safety rules |
| **Data poisoning** | Malicious data slipped into training sets can corrupt the model's outputs |
| **Model theft** | Competitors can probe your AI's API to reverse-engineer your proprietary logic |
| **Privacy violations** | AI can "memorize" sensitive info (credit cards, patient notes) and echo it back |
| **Autonomous misuse** | AI agents can be hijacked to send phishing emails or spam on your behalf |
| **Model bias** | Biased outputs (hiring, lending) carry legal penalties — 70% of firms are unprepared |

::: {.aside}
SentinelOne (2026). "AI Risk Mitigation: Tools and Strategies for 2026."
:::

# Live Demo {.section-header}

---

## Demo: Invoice Collections Analysis

**Let's walk through C.O.R.E. on a real billing dataset**

- ~50,000 billing transactions
- Each C.O.R.E. step as a separate prompt
- Analyzing late payment patterns

---

## Demo: Cash Flow Forecasting

**Now let's forecast cash inflows**

- Small business financial projections template
- All four C.O.R.E. steps in a single prompt
- Scenario analysis: what if revenue drops 20%?

# Takeaways {.section-header}

---

## What to Watch Out For

| Risk | Key Fact |
|------|---------|
| **Hallucinations** | Deloitte case — fabricated court quotes (Probert, 2026) |
| **Data leakage** | 68% of organizations affected (SentinelOne, 2026) |
| **"Silent AI" liability** | Insurance may not cover AI errors (Probert, 2026) |
| **Weak governance** | Only 17% prioritize compliance benchmarks (McKinsey, 2025) |

---

## 3 Things to Try This Week

1. **Anonymize a file and practice D.A.T.A. prompts**
   - Remove names/IDs, upload to ChatGPT or Claude *(Jasimuddin, 2026)*

2. **Add a self-critique prompt to any AI output**
   - "Audit this for errors or unsupported assumptions" *(Jasimuddin, 2026)*

3. **Document your AI tools**
   - List every AI tool and dataset your business uses *(SentinelOne, 2026)*

---

## Resources

**GitHub Repo:** [github.com/LJKelly3141/crp-webinar-ai](https://github.com/LJKelly3141/crp-webinar-ai)

- Slides, data, prompts, and financial template — all free to download
- Click the green **Code** button → **Download ZIP** (no account needed)

**Contact:** Dr. Logan Kelly | University of Wisconsin–River Falls

---

## References {.smaller}

1. Badmus & Ekoh (2026). "The Impact of AI on Business Growth in SMEs." *IRE Journals*.
2. David (2026). "AI in performance management." *MiHCM*.
3. Gonzalez de Villaumbrosia (2026). "15 AI Business Use Cases in 2026." *Product School*.
4. Jasimuddin (2026). "How FP&A Professionals Should Prompt AI in 2026." *FP&A Trends*.
5. Mayer et al. (2025). "AI in the workplace." *McKinsey & Company*.
6. Mehta (2026). "AI in Business: 7 Examples." *Crescendo.ai*.
7. Perlstein (2026). "6 AI-powered Market Trend Analysis Tools." *Revuze*.
8. Probert (2026). "How AI Is Transforming the Consulting Industry." *Whitehat SEO*.
9. SentinelOne (2026). "AI Risk Mitigation: Tools and Strategies for 2026."
10. Stellium Consulting (2026). "2026 AI Trends: What Enterprises Need to Know."

---

## Q&A {.center}

Thank you!
